# NSTC Guidelines

## Context

NSTC proposals often require careful compliance with local administrative formats in addition to the scientific narrative. Treat the official call and forms as the governing source.

## Typical Expectations

- Clear technical objective and expected contribution
- Feasible execution plan
- Evidence of prior preparation or preliminary work
- Bilingual or localized administrative components when required
- Appropriate fit with national or institutional priorities

## Writing Guidance

- Keep the technical objective concrete and measurable
- Explain novelty without over-claiming
- Show feasibility through prior results, infrastructure, or partnerships
- Make diagrams and work packages easy to scan

## Common Weaknesses

- Technical content is sound but the administrative package is incomplete
- Proposal is too broad for the budget or period
- Contribution is described generically rather than against a specific baseline
- Visual presentation is weak and makes the plan hard to follow

## Final NSTC Checklist

- Required forms are complete and internally consistent
- English and local-language summaries match each other
- Milestones, staffing, and budget align with the proposal scope
- Required diagrams and attachments are present
